statements.impact_statement: MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This SM2 decryption buffer overflow vulnerability requires processing untrusted SM2 ciphertext, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. OpenSSL certificate-parsing vulnerabilities are not exploitable in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. OpenSSL vulnerabilities are not exploitable in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. Certificate processing from untrusted sources does not occur, making this infinite-loop vulnerability unexploitable.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This memory leak vulnerability is not exploitable in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. Custom cipher usage from untrusted sources does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This buffer overflow vulnerability is not exploitable in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This buffer overflow vulnerability is not exploitable in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. The double-locking issue is not triggerable via external input in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. External GeneralName certificate parsing does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. Untrusted X.509 certificate chains with policy constraints are not processed in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. Untrusted key/IV inputs from external sources are not processed in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This SSL_free_buffers() use-after-free vulnerability requires active TLS connections from external sources, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. The SSL_select_next_proto vulnerability requires attacker-controlled input not present in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This X.509 certificate name check vulnerability requires processing certificates from external sources, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This CMS AuthEnvelopedData parsing vulnerability requires processing malicious CMS/PKCS#7 messages, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This PKCS#12 file parsing vulnerability requires processing malicious PKCS#12 files, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This TimeStamp Response verification vulnerability requires processing RFC 3161 timestamp responses, which does not occur in this deployment.

MongoDB is deployed on an isolated Docker internal network with no port exposed to the host in production, and does not have TLS configured. This PKCS#12 file parsing vulnerability requires processing malicious PKCS#12 files, which does not occur in this deployment.
